api/spec/packages/legacy/src/info.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for static v1 lookup endpoints: the supported-currencies list and async-operation progress tracking. A small self-contained read-only sub-package with no mutable resources and no pagination.

patterns.name: Flat model for static lookup entities
patterns.description: Currency and Progress are plain models with no Resource spread (no id/timestamps) because they are not persisted lifecycle entities. listCurrencies returns a flat Currency[] array, never PaginatedResponse.

patterns.name: routes.tsp holds interfaces, model file holds the model
patterns.description: CurrenciesEndpoints lives in routes.tsp; the Currency model lives in currencies.tsp; ProgressEndpoints and Progress co-locate in progress.tsp. main.tsp only imports the three files.
patterns.example: // currencies.tsp: model Currency only; routes.tsp: interface CurrenciesEndpoints

patterns.name: Progress id is a plain string, not ULID
patterns.description: getProgress takes id: string (not ULID) to match the progressmanager domain identifiers.

key_file_guides.role: CurrenciesEndpoints interface at /api/v1/info/currencies; single GET returning Currency[].
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns a plain array, not PaginatedResponse — do not add pagination here without a spec change.

key_file_guides.role: ProgressEndpoints interface (GET by id) and the Progress model (success/failed/total/updatedAt).
key_file_guides.watch_for: id is a plain string, not ULID — matches the progressmanager domain.

key_file_guides.role: Currency model with code (CurrencyCode), name, symbol, subunits (uint32).
key_file_guides.watch_for: subunits is uint32; zero-subunit currencies (JPY) are valid.

key_file_guides.role: Import manifest only — imports currencies.tsp, progress.tsp, routes.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files must be added here to be compiled.

anti_patterns: Adding a paginated response to listCurrencies — it returns all currencies as a flat array.
Adding mutating (POST/PUT/DELETE) operations — this folder is read-only lookup data.
Typing the Progress id as ULID instead of string.

decisions.decision: Currency and Progress are flat models with no Resource/timestamp spread.
decisions.rationale: They are static lookup/transient data, not persisted lifecycle entities, so id and timestamps are unnecessary.

code_examples.scenario: Static lookup list endpoint returning a flat array (not paginated)

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/notificatio.purpose: TypeSpec source for the v1 Notifications API — webhook channels, rules (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice created/updated), and event/delivery-status tracking. Discriminated unions on NotificationRule and NotificationChannel give type-safe multi-variant webhook rules.

patterns.name: Discriminated union (envelope:none, discriminator type) for polymorphic types
patterns.description: NotificationRule, NotificationRuleCreateRequest, NotificationChannel, NotificationChannelCreateRequest, and NotificationEventPayload are all @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }). Adding a rule type requires updating NotificationRule, NotificationRuleCreateRequest, and NotificationEventPayload together.

patterns.name: NotificationRuleCommon<T> / NotificationChannelCommon<T> generic base spread
patterns.description: Every rule model spreads ...NotificationRuleCommon<NotificationEventType.X> to inherit id, type literal, name, disabled, channels, annotations, metadata. Channel models spread ...NotificationChannelCommon<NotificationChannelType.X>. Never duplicate these fields.

patterns.name: CreateRequest via OmitProperties + re-added writable refs
patterns.description: Create-request models use @withVisibility(Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) and OmitProperties<Rule, "channels" | "features">, then re-add channels: Array<ULID> and features?: Array<ULIDOrKey> as ID-only writable forms.
patterns.example: @withVisibility(Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) model NotificationRuleBalanceThresholdCreateRequest { ...OmitProperties<NotificationRuleBalanceThreshold, "channels" | "features">; channels: Array<ULID>; features?: Array<ULIDOrKey>; }

patterns.name: Payload models carry full denormalized context
patterns.description: Entitlement payloads spread ...NotificationEventEntitlementValuePayloadBase to embed entitlement, feature, subject, value and optional customer inline. Invoice payloads embed the full Invoice. Webhook consumers need no second API call.

patterns.name: Backward-compat enum casing with #suppress
patterns.description: NotificationEventType values are dotted lowercase (entitlements.balance.threshold), NotificationChannelType.webhook maps to "WEBHOOK", and order-by enums use camelCase values — all carry #suppress "@openmeter/api-spec-legacy/casing" for backward compatibility.

patterns.name: PUT (not PATCH) updates with the create-request body
patterns.description: updateNotificationRule and updateNotificationChannel use @put and accept the full NotificationRuleCreateRequest / NotificationChannelCreateRequest schema — there is no partial-update body.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationChannelType enum, NotificationChannelMeta/Common/Webhook models, NotificationChannel union, and NotificationChannelsEndpoints CRUD interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: webhook value is uppercase "WEBHOOK" with a #suppress casing annotation — preserve for backward compat. signingSecret has a strict @pattern.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationRuleMeta/Common, the NotificationRule and NotificationRuleCreateRequest unions, and NotificationRulesEndpoints (CRUD plus a /{ruleId}/test POST).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Updates use PUT with the create-request body, not PATCH. A new rule type must be added to both unions here plus NotificationEventPayload in event.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: Entitlement rule/payload models: NotificationRuleBalanceThreshold, NotificationRuleEntitlementReset, payload bases, FeatureMeta, and NotificationRuleBalanceThresholdValueType.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NotificationRuleBalanceThresholdValueType has deprecated PERCENT/NUMBER variants — keep them, add new variants alongside (balance_value/usage_percentage/usage_value).

key_file_guides.role: Generic NotificationEventInvoicePayload<T>, the created/updated payload aliases, and NotificationRuleInvoiceCreated/Updated rule models plus their create requests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Invoice payloads embed the full billing Invoice model — ensure billing imports resolve. Create requests only re-add channels: Array<ULID> (no features).

key_file_guides.role: NotificationEventType enum, NotificationEvent model, delivery-status/attempt models, NotificationEventPayload union, NotificationEventResendRequest, and NotificationEventsEndpoints (list/get/resend).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All event-type values are dotted lowercase with #suppress for casing — maintain for new types. Delivery-status models live here, not in channel.tsp.

anti_patterns: Adding a rule type to NotificationRule without also updating NotificationRuleCreateRequest and NotificationEventPayload.
Putting delivery-status logic in channel.tsp — those models belong in event.tsp.
Using PATCH for updates — the API uses PUT with the full create-request body.
Inlining feature/channel lists instead of spreading FeatureMeta and NotificationChannelMeta.
Dropping the #suppress casing annotations on existing enum values — breaks backward compatibility.

decisions.decision: Create requests use OmitProperties to strip embedded objects and re-add ID-only writable fields.
decisions.rationale: Prevents clients from supplying server-assigned full channel/feature objects while keeping the read model as the single source of truth.

decisions.decision: Payload models embed full denormalized context (entitlement, feature, subject, customer, invoice).
decisions.rationale: Webhook consumers should act on a notification without a second API call — payloads are self-contained.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new notification rule type end-to-end

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/productcata.purpose: TypeSpec source for the v1 product-catalog, subscription, and add-on API contracts. Defines all models, enums, unions and route interfaces compiled into api/openapi.yaml and Go server stubs — only edit these .tsp files, never the generated outputs.

patterns.name: Discriminated unions with envelope:none
patterns.description: All polymorphic types (Price, RateCard, RateCardUsageBasedPrice, FeatureUnitCost, SubscriptionEditOperation) use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }). SubscriptionEditOperation uniquely uses "op" as the discriminator, not "type".
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Price { flat: FlatPrice, unit: UnitPrice, tiered: TieredPrice, dynamic: DynamicPrice, package: PackagePrice }

patterns.name: Per-field @visibility lifecycle annotation
patterns.description: Every concrete model field carries an explicit @visibility(...). Server-computed fields (status, effectiveFrom, effectiveTo, version, validationErrors, annotations) are Lifecycle.Read only. Only abstract base models (RateCardBase<T>) may omit visibility on some fields.

patterns.name: Spread-based composition over extends
patterns.description: Models compose by spreading shared bases (...UniqueResource, ...ResourceTimestamps, ...RateCardBase<T>, ...SpendCommitments, ...FlatPrice) instead of TypeSpec extends — enabling OmitProperties overrides in create variants.
patterns.example: model RateCardFlatFee { ...RateCardBase<RateCardType.flatFee>; billingCadence: duration | null; price: FlatPriceWithPaymentTerm | null; }

patterns.name: Create/update variants via OmitProperties + withVisibility
patterns.description: Write models (PlanAddonCreate, SubscriptionAddonCreate) use @withVisibility(Lifecycle.Create) and OmitProperties<DefaultKeyVisibility<Model, Lifecycle.Read>, "field"> to strip read-only fields and replace embedded objects with just an ID. Never duplicate fields from scratch.

patterns.name: All routes declared exclusively in routes.tsp
patterns.description: Every HTTP interface (FeaturesEndpoints, PlansEndpoints, PlanAddonsEndpoints, AddonsEndpoints, SubscriptionsEndpoints) is declared only in routes.tsp with @route/@tag/@friendlyName/@operationId/@summary. No other file declares route interfaces.
patterns.example: @route("/api/v1/plans") @tag("Product Catalog") interface PlansEndpoints { @get @operationId("listPlans") list(...): PaginatedResponse<Plan> | CommonErrors; }

patterns.name: Subscription mutations use CommonSubscriptionErrors
patterns.description: Subscription create/edit/change/cancel routes use CommonSubscriptionErrors (errors.tsp) carrying SubscriptionBadRequestErrorResponse/SubscriptionConflictErrorResponse with validationErrors[]. Feature/plan/addon routes use the parent CommonErrors alias.

patterns.name: ISO8601 encoding on all duration fields
patterns.description: Every duration field uses @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) with an @example(duration.fromISO("P1M")). One-time-fee cadence is typed duration | null. Omitting the encoding defaults to seconds serialization.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file declaring all HTTP interfaces; imports rest.tsp, subscriptionaddon.tsp, errors.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing @operationId/@friendlyName breaks Go codegen naming. Subscription mutations must use CommonSubscriptionErrors, not CommonErrors.

key_file_guides.role: RateCardBase<T> generic, RateCard union (flat_fee | usage_based), flat/usage rate cards, RateCardUsageBasedPrice union, and entitlement templates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new RateCard variant requires updating both the RateCard union and RateCardUsageBasedPrice union. Entitlement templates strip featureKey/featureId/usagePeriod via OmitProperties.

key_file_guides.role: Price union, PriceType enum, all price models (Flat/Unit/Tiered/Dynamic/Package) and their *WithCommitments variants spreading SpendCommitments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new price type must be added to PriceType enum, the Price union, AND RateCardUsageBasedPrice in ratecards.tsp — missing any one leaves the type unreachable from rate cards.

key_file_guides.role: Subscription/Expanded, phase/item/edit models, SubscriptionCreate and SubscriptionChange @oneOf unions, and all SubscriptionEditOperation variants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SubscriptionEditOperation uses discriminatorPropertyName: "op", not "type". @oneOf union variants need @summary.

key_file_guides.role: Plan, PlanPhase, PlanStatus, SettlementMode, PlanOrderBy, ProRatingConfig usage, PlanReference(Input).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plan.phases has @minItems(1); Plan.validationErrors is ValidationError[] | null (never optional). billingCadence/duration require ISO8601 encoding.

key_file_guides.role: CommonSubscriptionErrors alias and the subscription-specific error response models with SubscriptionErrorExtensions.validationErrors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Imports ratecards.tsp — avoid creating a circular import if ratecards.tsp later needs error types.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point importing sub-files in dependency order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: features.tsp and subscriptionaddon.tsp are intentionally NOT imported here (used via parent package / routes.tsp chain). Importing features.tsp here causes duplicate-namespace conflicts.

key_file_guides.role: Feature/FeatureCreateInputs models plus the FeatureUnitCost discriminated union (manual/llm) and resolved LLM pricing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: meterGroupByFilters is deprecated in favor of advancedMeterGroupByFilters. FeatureLLMUnitCost.pricing is Lifecycle.Read only.

anti_patterns: Defining a polymorphic type without @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", ... }) — produces a wrapped discriminator that breaks client deserialization.
Adding a route interface in any file other than routes.tsp — breaks @tag grouping and operationId consistency.
Duplicating fields for create/update variants instead of OmitProperties<DefaultKeyVisibility<Model, Lifecycle.Read>, "..."> + @withVisibility.
Using a duration field without @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) — defaults to seconds serialization.
Using CommonErrors instead of CommonSubscriptionErrors for subscription mutation routes — drops validationErrors from the schema.

decisions.rationale: Distinguishes PATCH command operations from resource-type discriminators used in Price/RateCard, avoiding naming collisions in generated code.

decisions.decision: PlanAddonCreate / SubscriptionAddonCreate replace the embedded Addon object with just { id } / addonId on write.
decisions.rationale: Write operations only need the identifier; embedding the read-only Addon would force clients to supply server-computed fields like status and version.

decisions.decision: CommonSubscriptionErrors is a local alias rather than reusing parent CommonErrors.
decisions.rationale: Subscription mutations return error bodies with a validationErrors array (SubscriptionErrorExtensions) not present in generic error types.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new usage-based price type to the catalog
